data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-908a6d5d">
    <title>project-local plugin installs + remove ddx install --global</title>
    <description>
Bead 2 of 4 children under epic ddx-3562c48c. Depends on bead 1 (ddx-c3efa754).

Move plugin installs to project-local; route through skills.Install; reject home-rooted manifests; delete the install --global command.

REGISTRY (cli/internal/registry/installer.go):
- Add projectRoot string parameter to InstallPackage and SymlinkSkillsFromRoot (rename to InstallSkillsFromRoot).
- Extraction target (lines 61-78): change from ~/.ddx/plugins/<plugin>/ to <projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<plugin>/. ZERO writes to ~/.ddx/plugins/, ~/.agents/skills/, ~/.claude/skills/.
- Manifest with Root.Target starting '~/' returns non-nil error: 'FEAT-015: Root.Target must be project-relative; got <value> in package <name>; update the manifest to use a relative path'. Non-zero exit propagates through ddx install.
- DELETE the <projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<plugin> → ~/.ddx/plugins/<plugin> symlink (lines 83-91).
- DELETE symlinkSkills (lines 326-423), SymlinkSkillsFromRoot, pruneStaleSkillLinks. All callers route through skills.Install(os.DirFS(<projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<plugin>), projectRoot, opts).
- DELETE writes to ~/.agents/skills/, ~/.claude/skills/ in registry.go:80-81 and 107-108.

LOCAL INSTALL (cli/cmd/install.go:234-389, the installLocal function):
- Same treatment: extract to <projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<plugin>/.
- DELETE the legacy ~-symlink at line 318.
- Replace SymlinkSkillsFromRoot call with skills.Install.

REMOVE GLOBAL INSTALL:
- DELETE cli/cmd/install_global.go.
- DELETE cli/cmd/install_global_test.go.
- Remove --global flag from cli/cmd/install.go (cobra default error on unknown flag is acceptable).

COMMIT HYGIENE:
- commitPluginChanges: stage <projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<plugin>/ in addition to .agents/skills/ and .claude/skills/.

TESTS:
- TestSymlinkSkills_* (cli/internal/registry/registry_test.go:240-391) → rewrite as TestInstallSkills_*. TestSymlinkSkills_PluginRootSymlinkDoesNotEscape replaced with stronger test: walk all installed paths, assert (a) no symlinks, (b) no path resolves outside <projectRoot>.
- cli/cmd/installation_acceptance_test.go: rewrite to expect project-local layout (currently asserts ~/.ddx/plugins/ddx).
- NEW TestInstall_NoSymlinksCreated: post-install of 'ddx init && ddx install <plugin>', walk <projectRoot>/.agents/skills/, .claude/skills/, .ddx/plugins/<plugin>/ — assert ZERO symlinks anywhere. Locks the cross-platform invariant.
- NEW TestInstall_RejectsHomeRootedManifestTarget: package with Root.Target: ~/.ddx/plugins/x → InstallPackage returns the FEAT-015 error.
- NEW TestInstall_GlobalFlagRemoved: ddx install --global <pkg> exits non-zero with cobra unknown-flag error.
- state.Files records project-relative paths after install (assertion in updated TestInstallPackage_*).

OUT OF SCOPE: persona fallback, GraphQL pluginRootCandidates, doctor, ddx update behavior, .gitignore, docs. (Beads 3 and 4.)
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. registry.InstallPackage accepts projectRoot string. 2. Extraction target is <projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<plugin>/; zero writes to ~/.ddx/plugins/, ~/.agents/skills/, ~/.claude/skills/. 3. Manifest with ~-prefixed Root.Target returns the specified FEAT-015 error; ddx install exits non-zero. Verified by TestInstall_RejectsHomeRootedManifestTarget. 4. installLocal (install.go:234-389) does same; legacy ~-symlink at line 318 deleted. 5. symlinkSkills, SymlinkSkillsFromRoot, pruneStaleSkillLinks all deleted; callers route through skills.Install. 6. <projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<plugin> → ~/.ddx/plugins/<plugin> symlink no longer created. 7. cli/cmd/install_global.go and install_global_test.go deleted; --global flag removed from install.go. TestInstall_GlobalFlagRemoved passes. 8. commitPluginChanges stages .ddx/plugins/<plugin>/. 9. TestSymlinkSkills_* rewritten as TestInstallSkills_*; PluginRootSymlinkDoesNotEscape replaced with no-symlinks + no-escape walk. 10. installation_acceptance_test.go rewritten to project-local layout. 11. TestInstall_NoSymlinksCreated walks all three target trees and asserts zero symlinks. 12. state.Files records project-relative paths. 13. End-to-end: 'ddx init && ddx install helix' in tmp dir produces tree with zero symlinks. 14. cd cli && go build ./... && go test ./... passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:cli, area:registry, kind:feature, spec:FEAT-015</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-3562c48c" spec-id="FEAT-015" base-rev="3915587ca0468a6ff9930146ca265f3bade40530" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260429T143710-c87f5cd2"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-015" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-015-installation-architecture.md">Feature: DDx Installation Architecture</ref>
  </governing>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> sections above are the completion contract — every AC checkbox must be provably satisfied by a specific piece of code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## How to work

1. Read first. If the bead description names files, read them to see what is already there. If the bead references a spec, ADR, or API contract, read the relevant sections before writing code. Do not start editing until you understand what the bead wants changed and why.

2. Cross-reference your work against the acceptance criteria as you go. Before committing, walk through the AC one item at a time and identify the specific test name, file path, or function that satisfies each checkbox. If you cannot point to concrete evidence for an AC item, it is not done.

3. Run the project's verification commands before committing. Use whatever commands match the crate or package you touched — typically `cargo test` and `cargo clippy` for Rust, `bun test` or `npm test` for JS/TS, `go test` for Go. The bead description usually names the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If a test or lint fails, fix it first.

4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with `git add <specific-paths>` — never `git add -A`, because there may be unrelated WIP in this worktree. Use a conventional-commit-style subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. DDx will merge your commits back to the base branch.

5. If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write your reasoning to `.ddx/executions/20260429T143710-c87f5cd2/no_changes_rationale.txt` with: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up attempt would need. Do NOT commit partial, exploratory, or red code hoping the reviewer will accept it — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt, potentially with a stronger model.

## The bead contract overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. If the bead asks for new documentation, write it. If the bead adds a new module or crate, add it. Conservative rules (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.

## Quality bar and the review step

After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a `<review-findings>` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass so the bead closes cleanly.**

If this prompt already contains a `<review-findings>` section, address every BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Those findings are the precise list of what the previous attempt missed.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is already initialized. Running it corrupts the bead queue and project configuration.
- Do not modify files outside the scope the bead description names.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and you have verified every AC item, stop. Return control to the orchestrator. Do not continue to explore the repository, run extra tests, or generate follow-up notes — the bead is complete, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Bead complete. Summary of changes:

- **`InstallPackage(pkg, projectRoot)`**: new signature; rejects `~`-rooted `Root.Target` with FEAT-015 error; extracts to `<projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/`; routes skills through `skills.Install`; deleted legacy `~`-symlink and `symlinkSkills`/`pruneStaleSkillLinks` machinery.
- **`installLocal`**: copies plugin into project (no developer-checkout symlinks); rejects `~`-rooted targets; routes skills through `skills.Install`.
- **`--global` removed**: deleted `install_global.go` + test; removed flag from `install.go`.
- **`BuiltinRegistry`**: helix `Root.Target` now project-local; removed `~`-rooted skill mappings.
- **Tests**: `TestInstall_RejectsHomeRootedManifestTarget`, `TestInstall_NoSymlinksCreated`, `TestInstall_GlobalFlagRemoved`, `TestInstallSkills_NoSymlinksAnywhere`, `TestInstallLocalRejectsHomeRootedManifestTarget`; rewrote `TestInstallLocalPreservesExistingProjectPluginDirUnlessForced` for project-local layout; updated `installation_acceptance_test.go` to assert project-local plugin path.
- **Callers updated**: `init.go`, `update.go`, GraphQL resolver pass `workingDir` to `InstallPackage`.
